title: Mob Trims
description: Allows mobs to spawn with trimmed armor naturally
body:
# Mobs wearing armor trims   ![Logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e47b1cb278f87f8301f6d621e6d2c612cb4fc441.png)
## Updated release for Minecraft 26.1.2 using Fabric

This mob allows naturally spawned **mobs** to wear **trimmed armor**. 

Players can trim their armor using a smithing table... ![Trimmed armor](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/83efc9e4eafecbf4e100bbf8c39be88a7fa9a4c8.webp)
... so why shouldn't the mobs do so as well?

Probablity can be modified in the `mob_trims.json` config file.
You can also exclude specific armor trim materials as well as patterns.

![Skeleton wearing trimmed armor naturally](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/16d8141f819d1343316bdf2334cbde9848d6cc89.png)

An example configuration would be:

```json
{
  "probability": 0.75,
  "exclusions": {
    "materials": [],
    "patterns": [
      "minecraft:wild",
      "minecraft:vex"
    ]
  }
}
```
Armor on mobs will be trimmed with a 3/4 chance but wild and vex patterns would be excluded.
